Version No. 001
Education (Special Developmental Schools)
Act 1976
Act No. 8918/1976
Version incorporating amendments as at 25 November 1997
An Act to make Provision with respect to Members of the
Teaching Service at Special Developmental Schools.
BE IT ENACTED by the Queen's Most Excellent Majesty by
and with the advice and consent of the Legislative Council
and the Legislative Assembly of Victoria in this present
Parliament assembled and by the authority of the same as
follows (that is to say):
1. Short title and commencement
(1) This Act may be cited as the Education (Special
Developmental Schools) Act 1976.
(2) This Act shall come into operation on the day on
which it receives the Royal Assent.
2. Definitions
In this Act unless inconsistent with the context or
subject-matter—
"special developmental school" means any S. 2 def. of
"special
special school within the meaning of
develop-
Division 2 of Part IV of the Education Act mental
school"
1958 which is a school for intellectually
substituted by
handicapped children registered as a day No. 9714
s. 88(a)(i).
training centre under the Mental Health Act
1959 for which the Education Department
provides teachers or general educational
facilities;

Nos 9714 Notwithstanding anything in any Act in
s. 88(b)(i)(ii),
10029 s. 23. computing the period of service of a member of
the teaching service provided by the Education
Department at a special developmental school
who immediately prior to being so provided was a
teacher at a place registered as a day training
centre under the Mental Health Act 1959 for the
purpose of determining any entitlement to long
service leave or sick leave there shall be taken into
account to the extent approved by the Minister in
any case any service as a teacher at any place
registered as aforesaid and any service so
approved shall be deemed to be continuous with
any other service required to be taken into account
for the purposes aforesaid.

1. General Information
The Education (Special Developmental Schools) Act 1976 was assented to

section 1(2).
